name: Guerrilla Jiu-Jitsu Modesto
tagline: The #1 Jiu-Jitsu Academy in Modesto, CA

home.heroHeadline: The #1 Jiu-Jitsu Academy in Modesto, CA
home.heroSubheadline: We teach you how to become an expert at self-defense using Jiu-Jitsu.

classesOverview.name: Adults Jiu-Jitsu (NoGi)
classesOverview.description: The only NoGi-specialty school in the area. Beginners program covers the key components and systems of submission grappling. Advanced class goes deeper into finer details, options, and trap-setting — whether you are new or experienced, there is a program for you.

classesOverview.name: Youth / Kids Jiu-Jitsu
classesOverview.description: Our kids program helps your child become an expert at self-defense using our proven system. Classes for kids age 3 and up. Whether they want self-defense, fitness and friends, or to compete in grappling tournaments — we have a program for them.

testimonials.name: Irving Garcia
testimonials.quote: My younger brother and I recently went to a trial class and it was great. Mike, the instructor, was super friendly, welcoming, and supportive. The students were very nice as well. The place is clean and in a great location. We got a membership the same day. 10/10.

testimonials.name: Miles Martinez
testimonials.quote: This place is the real deal for anyone looking to begin Jiu-Jitsu or level up. Mike and Hani are some of the best in the area and put their all into their students. It's a great environment for families and children. Guerrilla Jiu Jitsu Modesto is the only place I'd recommend in the area.

testimonials.name: John Peery
testimonials.quote: I've been to a couple of Jiu-Jitsu schools around town and these guys are by far the best. I've been training there for 9 months and my son started in January. We've both progressed so much. The curriculum is top notch, classes are not intimidating, and the vibe is supportive and positive.

about.heading: About Guerrilla Jiu-Jitsu Modesto
credentials: Nine years of professional Jiu-Jitsu experience

additionalTestimonials.name: D Ventura
additionalTestimonials.quote: This is an excellent place to train whether you are an adult, teenager, or child. Exceptional instruction and a safe environment where everyone can grow mentally and physically strong.

additionalTestimonials.name: Chris Vloothuis
additionalTestimonials.quote: Great place to train — supportive staff, clean facility, and instruction that actually makes you better fast.

additionalTestimonials.name: Natasha Fraser
additionalTestimonials.quote: My kids love it here. Instructors are patient and the community is like family.

faqs.q: Is Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu suitable for beginners?
faqs.a: Yes, Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu is suitable for beginners of all ages and fitness levels.

faqs.q: Do I need to be in shape to start training Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu?
faqs.a: No, Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu is for everyone regardless of current fitness level.

faqs.q: I'm worried about getting injured. Is Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu safe?
faqs.a: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu emphasizes technique and control, reducing the risk of injury.

faqs.q: Can Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu help with self-defense?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu teaches effective self-defense techniques for individuals of all sizes and strengths.

faqs.q: What can I expect in my first Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu class?
faqs.a: In your first class, you'll start with a warm-up, learn foundational techniques, and drill them with a partner in a welcoming environment.

faqs.q: What should I wear to a NoGi class?
faqs.a: Athletic shorts (no zippers or pockets) and a rashguard or fitted t-shirt. No shoes on the mats.

faqs.q: Do you offer a free trial?
faqs.a: Yes — your first class is free. Book a time that works for you and we'll take care of the rest.
Yes. We have kids classes for ages 3 and up, grouped by age for a safe, age-appropriate experience.

legal.privacyPolicy: Standard privacy policy — we collect contact form submissions to respond to inquiries and for marketing communications. No data sold to third parties.
legal.termsConditions: Standard terms — membership agreements governed by signed waiver and liability release.

facility.description: No interior/facility shots found on current site

designReasoning: Modern BJJ academy with a strong accessible/family-friendly positioning (kids age 3+, beginner-welcoming tone), lineage credentials (black belt under Alejandro Perez, mentored by Dave Camarillo) but no pro fighter roster, and a clearly sport-brand vibe. Performance-Athletic is the best fit — bold sportswear energy, kinetic marquees, electric accent — using their extracted gold (#AE9958) and red (#EF1921) as the palette.
currentSiteIssues: Built on a GoHighLevel template — looks generic and templated. Weak hero hierarchy (two stacked H1s, competing CTAs). No clear visual proof (no real instructor photos, no mat/facility shots). Typography is flat, spacing is uneven, color palette is mostly black/white with gold accent under-leveraged. Testimonials are buried and unstyled. Schedule page is a plain text block (no visual grid or filtering). FAQs lack accordion structure. Blog is empty. No visible lead-capture flow — CTAs go to a basic external scheduler.
